Captured clips

The mic pre-records continuously into a rolling buffer — like a cinema camera. Each press of the corner button saves a snapshot of what was said before and after the press, so read-ons and read-offs get captured even without a mic pack.

Export paperwork

Generates filled PDFs from the official blank forms. On your phone, they land in Downloads — share/save them to Google Drive from there.

Certificate details

Pre-filled from the job; tweak before exporting if needed.

Audio

Read statements

Placeholders: {DEPONENT} {CASE} {CAUSE} {DATE} {DATE_LONG} {TIME} {LOCATION} {ORDERED_BY} {CLIENT} {VIDEOGRAPHER} {JOB_NO}

Gmail connection (optional)

To pull assignments straight from Gmail you need a (free) Google OAuth Client ID:
1. Go to console.cloud.google.com → create a project.
2. Enable the Gmail API (APIs & Services → Library).
3. APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen → External → add yourself as a test user.
4. Credentials → Create Credentials → OAuth client ID → Web application → add this app's web address (its "origin", e.g. https://yourname.github.io) under Authorized JavaScript origins.
5. Paste the Client ID below. Until then, copy-paste works great.

The Google sign-in asks for two permissions: read Gmail (to find assignments) and manage files this app creates in Drive (for backups and PDF exports — it cannot see the rest of your Drive).

Google Drive auto-sync

Everything dumps into the Naegeli Job Assistant folder in your Drive automatically — a subfolder per job number holding its job-#.json, its audio clips, and its exported PDFs. On another device, tap the ☁ up top and your jobs load right in. Typing a job number also pulls that job's file from Drive if a newer one exists.

Identity

Sign white paper with a dark pen, snap a photo or scan it, and upload. It gets placed on the certificate's signature line automatically.

Danger zone
